data.title: Investors draw $1.82B from Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs, as precious metals rally
data.description:
Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs have registered negative flows totaling $1. 82 billion in the last week amid a precious metals rally.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/1-82b-from-bitcoin-and-ethereum-etfs/

data.title: Over the past hour, more than $50 million in liquidations have occurred across the entire network, with ETH liquidations reaching $19.98 million.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, approximately $54.07 million in positions were liquidated across the network in the past hour, including approximately $52.6 million in long positions and approximately $1.47 million in short positions. In addition, $19.98 million in ETH positions were liquidated and $10.97 million in BTC positions were liquidated.

data.title: Trading expert sets date when Bitcoin will hit $145,000
data.description:
A trading analyst has outlined a long-term timeline for Bitcoin’s (BTC) next major price milestone, arguing that the cryptocurrency is unlikely to reach $145,000 until the current market cycle has fully reset. According to TradingShot, recent developments on high-timeframe technical indicators suggest Bitcoin has entered the early stages of a broader bear cycle, as outlined in a TradingView analysis on January 30. The assessment is grounded in quarterly chart signals, where Bitcoin has recently moved past a Relative Vigor Index (RVGI) bearish cross. On higher timeframes, the RVGI is viewed as a cycle-defining indicator rather than a short-term momentum signal
source: https://finbold.com/trading-expert-sets-date-when-bitcoin-will-hit-145000/

data.title: RIVER sell-off deepens – Will prices dip further as liquidity drains?
data.description:

source: https://ambcrypto.com/river-sell-off-deepens-will-price-dip-further-as-liquidity-drains/

data.title: A whale who accumulated 54.731 million W tokens three weeks ago is suspected of liquidating their holdings and will face a loss of approximately $650,000.
data.description: According to Arkham's monitoring, a whale recently transferred all 54.731 million W tokens to a Coinbase hot wallet address, worth approximately $1.36 million. These tokens were accumulated by the whale three weeks ago and were worth over $2.016 million at the time. Selling them now would result in a loss of $653,800.

data.description:

data.title: Cango mined 108.1 BTC this week, bringing its total Bitcoin holdings to 7,981.7.
data.description: Cango, a US-listed Bitcoin mining company, announced on the X platform that it mined 108.1 BTC this week, bringing its total Bitcoin holdings to approximately 7,981.7 BTC, with a current deployed hashrate of 50 EH/s.

data.title: Which Crypto Exchanges Dominated Spot Trading in 2025?
data.description:
Binance remained the largest centralized cryptocurrency exchange in December 2025, according to new data published by CoinGecko. Despite a sharp month-over-month slowdown in activity, Binance closed the year with a 38. 3% share of global spot trading volume, maintaining its dominant position among centralized exchanges
source: https://www.ethnews.com/which-crypto-exchanges-dominated-spot-trading-in-2025/

data.title: Warsh's Fed Chair Nomination May Impact Precious Metals
data.description: Seth R. Freeman from GlassRatner Advisory highlights that the nomination of Warsh as Fed Chair aims to restore market confidence following tensions between U.S. President Donald Trump and Powell. According to NS3.AI, Warsh's hawkish stance is expected to strengthen the dollar, negatively impacting precious metals like gold and silver. Traders heavily invested in unhedged long positions in precious metals may face significant losses in the near term.

data.title: Analysis: The "Wash Shock" will continue next week, and traders heavily invested in precious metals may find themselves in trouble.
data.description: Seth R. Freeman, Senior Managing Director at GlassRatner Consulting & Capital Group, points out that one of Warsh's primary tasks as the newly nominated Federal Reserve Chair will be rebuilding credibility in global markets. This is particularly important given the tense relationship between Trump and Powell in recent months. Freeman also states that Warsh is the best candidate, given his long experience in government. The best news is that we no longer have to deal with the uncertainty and market turmoil caused by Trump's constant pressure on the Fed Chair, which is good for everyone. Furthermore, he notes that the sharp drop in gold and the even more dramatic decline in silver indicate a stronger dollar and a different environment, especially for silver. Given this nomination, it wouldn't be surprising if metal prices don't rebound significantly. And given Warsh's hawkish leanings, traders heavily invested in precious metals may face losses, especially those with unhedged or short positions. Some traders may find themselves in serious trouble next Monday, at least those who haven't taken a neutral stance or shorted precious metals. (Jinshi)

data.title: NYSE and Nasdaq to Launch 24/7 Trading for Tokenized Stocks
data.description: The New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) and Nasdaq are set to implement round-the-clock trading for tokenized stocks, aiming to tackle the issue of limited liquidity during weekends. According to NS3.AI, this strategic move is seen as a major advancement for the market. Ondo Finance President Ian de Bode highlighted the potential benefits of this initiative, which could increase market accessibility and enhance trading conditions for tokenized assets.

data.title: Step Finance Experiences Security Breach Affecting Multiple Wallets
data.description: Step Finance has reported a security breach affecting several of its treasury and fee wallets. According to Odaily, blockchain data reveals that during the attack, approximately 261,854 SOL tokens were unstaked and transferred. Step Finance has stated that they are currently investigating the incident and will provide further information in due course.

data.title: Fed Chair Nominee Waller Expected to Restore Market Confidence
data.description:
Seth R. Freeman, Senior Managing Director at GlassRatner Advisory & Capital Group, highlighted that one of the primary tasks for the newly nominated Federal Reserve Chair, Waller, will be to restore global market confidence. According to Odaily, this is particularly significant given the recent tensions between U.S. President Donald Trump and Jerome Powell over the past few months.

Freeman expressed that Waller is the best choice for the position, having served in government for a long time. The positive aspect of this nomination is the anticipated reduction in uncertainty and market disruptions caused by Trump's persistent pressure on the Federal Reserve Chair, which is beneficial for everyone.

Additionally, Freeman noted the sharp decline in gold and an even steeper drop in silver, indicating a stronger dollar and a different market environment, especially for silver. Given this nomination, it should not be surprising if metal prices do not rebound significantly. With Waller's hawkish stance, traders heavily invested in precious metals might face losses, particularly those with unhedged or short positions. Some traders could find themselves in a difficult situation next Monday, especially those not maintaining a neutral stance or shorting precious metals.
